name: Via Serrana: Olvera and Setenil Loop Trail
name_original: Ruta de la Zurrapa – circular entre Olvera y Setenil
description: This 25.8-mile moderate mountain biking loop connects Olvera and Setenil, offering varied terrain and views of white villages and griffon vu
region_intro: Mountain biking around Olvera offers varied terrain, from the accessible greenways to the more challenging natural park trails. The region is characterized by the Vía Verde de la Sierra, a disused railway line transformed into a cycling path, featuring tunnels, viaducts, and views of olive groves and river gorges. Adjacent to Olvera, the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park presents rolling hills and diverse landscapes for mountain bikers seeking more demanding routes. This area provides a mix of compacted soil paths and natural trails through lush valleys and alongside rivers. Best mountain bike trails around Olvera The most popular mountain bike trail is Via Serrana: Olvera and Setenil Loop Trail, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects Olvera with the charming white village of Setenil de las Bodegas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vía Verde de la Sierra – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Olvera, a moderate 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This route follows the scenic greenway, passing through tunnels and over viaducts with views of the surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo Tunnel – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Antigua estación de Navalagrulla, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde de la Sierra, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. This moderate trail offers a journey through the region's natural and historical features. Mountain biking around Olvera is defined by greenways, rolling hills, and olive groves, with routes often passing through natural parks and alongside rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, largely flat paths to more challenging climbs. The routes in Olvera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Olvera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Olvera?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails available around Olvera, offering a range of experiences from easy greenways to more challenging routes through natural parks. You'll find 2 easy routes, 8 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Olvera?
A: The region around Olvera, including the Vía Verde de la Sierra and Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, is generally pleasant for mountain biking outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for exploring the trails.

Q: What is the general terrain like for mountain biking around Olvera?
A: The terrain around Olvera is quite varied. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10297017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offers largely flat paths with gentle inclines, consisting of compacted soil and well-maintained asphalt. Beyond the greenway, routes in the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park feature rolling hills, diverse landscapes, and more demanding climbs and descents on natural trails.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural features to see while mountain biking near Olvera?
A: Absolutely. While cycling, you can encounter numerous points of interest. Along the Vía Verde de la Sierra, you'll pass through 30 tunnels and over four impressive viaducts, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3795122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaframagón Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. A significant natural highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, home to one of Europe's largest griffon vulture colonies. You can also explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7520502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Olvera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails around Olvera?
A: The mountain bike trails around Olvera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders frequently praise the scenic beauty of the Vía Verde de la Sierra, the diverse terrain, and the opportunity to experience both accessible greenways and more challenging natural park routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Olvera?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra – Olvera greenway loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for families. Its largely flat and well-maintained surface makes it suitable for riders of varying skill levels, including beginners and children. The route offers a relaxed ride through picturesque landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Olvera?
A: Yes, several circular routes are popular in the area. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994508324/via-serrana-olvera-and-setenil-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Serrana: Olvera and Setenil Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; connects Olvera with the charming village of Setenil de las Bodegas. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10297017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the scenic greenway.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while mountain biking in the region?
A: The region is rich in wildlife, particularly along the Vía Verde de la Sierra and within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. A major highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, where you can observe one of Europe's largest populations of griffon vultures. The routes also pass through untouched countryside, offering glimpses of various birds and other local fauna.

Q: Is parking available for mountain bikers in Olvera?
A: Yes, facilities at the starting stations of the Vía Verde de la Sierra, including the one in Olvera, typically offer parking options for visitors. These stations are designed to accommodate cyclists and provide convenient access to the trails.

Q: Can I rent mountain bikes in Olvera?
A: Yes, bike rentals are available at the starting stations of the Vía Verde de la Sierra, including the station in Olvera. You can often find both standard mountain bikes and electric options, ensuring you have the right equipment for your ride.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes, particularly within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14590194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra – Olvera Town Center loop from Olvera&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes and covers a distance of nearly 50 km.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails in Olvera?
A: Yes, the old Olvera Station, which serves as a starting point for the Vía Verde de la Sierra, often has facilities including cafes. Additionally, routes frequently pass through or near charming 'Pueblos Blancos' (white villages) where you can find local eateries and cafes to refuel.
tips:
Located about two kilometers south of the center of Olvera, this sanctuary is dedicated to the Virgin of Los Remedios, patron saint of Olvera.
The interior of the sanctuary is beautifully decorated with paintings. Next to the church, there is a beautiful view of the white village of Olvera.
A picturesque place with its wine cellars carved into the rock face, specialty shops and Schlemmerlokalen- and above all enthroned a former Moorish fortress
This white town has it all: a castle, several churches, steep alleyways and surprising corners full of flowers and plants.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Via Serrana: Olvera and Setenil Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied mountain biking experience. While sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra are generally smooth, with compacted soil or well-maintained asphalt, other parts of this loop, especially those connecting Olvera and Setenil, can be more technical. Expect a mix of gentle inclines, particularly when heading towards Olvera, and potentially some rough, rustic, and steeply uphill paths with wooden bridges in certain areas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for mountain biking this loop is around 3 hours and 44 minutes, covering a distance of approximately 41.5 kilometers (25.8 miles).

faqs.question: Is the Via Serrana: Olvera and Setenil Loop Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as 'moderate' and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills due to its varied terrain and some potentially challenging sections. While parts of the route follow the accessible Vía Verde de la Sierra, the full loop is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: A significant highlight for wildlife enthusiasts is the Peñón de Zaframagón, a Nature Reserve that hosts one of Europe's largest colonies of griffon vultures. You'll often see these majestic birds soaring overhead. The trail also passes through diverse natural landscapes, offering glimpses of local flora and fauna.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The loop connects two charming 'white villages', Olvera and Setenil de las Bodegas. In Olvera, you'll get dramatic views of its castle and baroque church. Setenil is famous for its unique cave houses. Other notable features include the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Remedies, the Bodega Under the Rock Overhang, Setenil de las Bodegas, and the Olvera Town Center. The route also features numerous tunnels and viaducts from the old railway line.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the towns of Olvera or Setenil de las Bodegas, which serve as excellent starting points for the loop. Both towns offer amenities and access to different sections of the trail.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural reserve areas like Peñón de Zaframagón, and when passing through towns or near livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather in Andalusia is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Via Serrana: Olvera and Setenil Loop Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra. It also passes through parts of the Via Serrana, Vereda de Pino, and Cordel del Arroyo o Río Salado, among other local paths.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the loop connects the towns of Olvera and Setenil de las Bodegas, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in both locations where you can refuel and enjoy local Andalusian cuisine.
